actual_coverage.reasoning: Both ask for multi-year drivers behind the growth algorithm (Perfecta/ROIC/yield/cost levers) and how that underpins durable growth.

Directly targets Med moderation, remaining inventory, and rebound assumptions embedded in guidance.
Both focus on booking trajectory versus prior period and close-in versus forward demand, including into 2027.

Actual asks construction status/resumption in Mexico, whereas the prediction asks economics/ramp timing; related asset but different ask.
Both probe how temporary the Europe headwinds are and the sensitivity/return in 2027 under normalized Med conditions.
Direct decomposition of yield growth between new hardware/private destinations versus like-for-like.
Actual asks about itinerary changes due to fuel and deferrals; prediction focuses on Perfecta savings and cost levers, not itinerary changes.
Both center on fuel hedging approach/sensitivity in a high fuel environment; the second part on NCC thresholds is not addressed.
Directly targets loyalty/credit card KPIs and monetization impacts on yield and guest behavior.
Actual is about North American airfare sensitivity; prediction focuses on Med/Europe air cost effects.

summary: Coverage was strong on Med booking dynamics, booking trajectory, yield decomposition, destination ramps, fuel hedging, and loyalty/card impacts (9/12 actuals covered, avg score ~2.83). Precision was moderate (57/110 useful; avg predicted best score ~2.05) with many overpredictions in capital allocation, JV/TUI, and ship-level economics that did not appear in the actual Q&A. Misses centered on Mexico construction status, fuel-driven itinerary changes/deferrals, and North American airfare demand effects.
